title: Command line statistics are double counted with `--stats`
body:
```
./t/t_EXAMPLE.py --no-skip-identical
```

yields:

```
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.043 devel rev v5.042-14-g428bd97ce
- Verilator: Built from 0.028 MB sources in 3 modules, into 0.022 MB in 5 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 0.011 s (elab=0.000, cvt=0.003, bld=0.000); cpu 0.011 s on 1 threads; alloced 28.922 MB
```

While

```
./t/t_EXAMPLE.py --no-skip-identical --stats
```

yields:

```
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.043 devel rev v5.042-14-g428bd97ce
- Verilator: Built from 0.052 MB sources in 3 modules, into 0.042 MB in 9 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 0.014 s (elab=0.002, cvt=0.004, bld=0.000); cpu 0.014 s on 1 threads; alloced 32.457 MB
```

Note the difference in the number and size of C++ files reported.

- Verilator: Walltime 0.014 s (elab=0.002, cvt=0.004, bld=0.000); cpu 0.014 s on 1 threads; alloced 32.457 MB
